classification_scheme: DPIIT's own legacy DIPP/SIA broad industry-group classification (~39 named categories, e.g. 'Metallurgical industries', 'Chemicals (other than fertilizers)', 'Fermentation industries', 'Cement & gypsum products', 'Food processing industry', 'Textiles', 'Drugs and pharmaceuticals', 'Sugar', 'Paper and pulp including paper products', 'Transportation Industry', plus an 'Others' bucket). This is NOT NIC 2-digit divisions; it is DPIIT's historical Secretariat for Industrial Assistance (SIA) industry grouping used for IEM/industrial-licensing statistics. Same category list is used for both Part A (filed/intended) and Part B (implemented/actual) tables.

totals.period: Jan-Dec 2023 (full calendar year); Jan-Aug 2024 partial-year Grand Totals are Part A 663 and Part B 826. Grand totals verified against Annexure-3/Annexure-7 'Grand Total' rows and the Part-A/Part-B year-wise summary tables.

notes: DEFINITIVE: DPIIT DOES publish IEMs by industry/sector. The IEM-vs-EC-by-activity comparison IS feasible from DPIIT data. Source = DPIIT monthly 'IEM STATISTICS REPORT', which contains both state-wise AND industry-wise breakdowns for Part A (filed/intended) and Part B (implemented/actual).

Classification is DPIIT's OWN legacy DIPP/SIA broad industry-group scheme (39 named categories + 'Others'), NOT NIC 2-digit divisions. The identical category list is used in every table (monthly Part A/B and all cumulative annexures). Exact labels transcribed as printed, including original spellings/spacing ('Electricals equipment', 'Chemicals (other than fertilizers)', 'Miscellaneous mechanical & engineering industries').

Each monthly report carries: (a) current-month Part A & Part B industry-wise tables, and (b) Annexures 3,4,7,8 giving CUMULATIVE industry-wise counts (Annex 3 = Part A count, Annex 7 = Part B count) and investment Rs cr (Annex 4 = Part A, Annex 8 = Part B), each with four columns: Jan-Dec 2022, Jan-Dec 2023, Jan-Aug 2023, Jan-Aug 2024. This JSON uses Jan-Dec 2023 (latest full year) and Jan-Aug 2024 (latest available). Jan-Dec 2022 and Jan-Aug 2023 columns are also available in the same annexures if a longer time series is wanted.

COUNTS vs VALUE: part_a_count and part_b_count are numbers of IEM acknowledgments (transcribed exactly from Annexures 3 and 7). investment_rs_cr in each row is the Part B (IMPLEMENTED/actual) total investment from Annexure-8; Part A (intended) investment is separately available in Annexure-4 and is generally larger (e.g. Jan-Dec 2023 Part A grand total = Rs 510,852 cr vs Part B Rs 276,925 cr).

Part A = IEM filed at project-intention stage (delicensed sectors, self-declaration). Part B = filed at commencement of commercial production (implemented). Only a subset of Part A projects ever file Part B, so Part B is the better proxy for capacity actually built and comparable to realized EC/consent events.

eia_ec_relevant is an INDICATIVE mapping to the EIA Notification 2006 Schedule, flagged true for categories that map to heavy/polluting scheduled activities: Metallurgical industries -> 3(a) Metallurgical (ferrous & non-ferrous); Cement & gypsum products -> 3(b) Cement plants; Chemicals (other than fertilizers) -> 5(f) Synthetic organic chemicals; Drugs and pharmaceuticals -> 5(f) (bulk drugs/API only, not formulation); Dye-stuffs -> 5(f); Fermentation industries -> 5(g) Distilleries; Fertilizers -> 5(a); Sugar -> 5(j) Sugar industry; Paper and pulp -> 5(i) Pulp & paper; Leather -> leather/skin/hide (tanneries); Fuels -> petroleum refining/coke/petrochemicals (Schedule cat 4/6). These flags are approximate: EC requirement is triggered by CAPACITY THRESHOLDS and sub-product, not the broad IEM label, so a large 'Food processing', 'Textiles' (bleaching/dyeing) or 'Glass'/'Ceramics' unit CAN cross an EC threshold while most units in those categories do not.

CAVEAT for mapping onto EC-by-activity: DPIIT's IEM industry categories are BROADER and differently drawn than the EIA Schedule activity codes. One IEM category can span several EC items (e.g. 'Chemicals (other than fertilizers)' covers synthetic organic chemicals 5(f), pesticides 5(b), petrochemicals, etc.), and some EC activities (mining, thermal power, ports, real estate, highways, river valley) are NOT industrial-licensing/IEM items at all and will have zero IEM footprint. The bridge is a many-to-many concordance, not 1:1.

IEM is a self-declaration for DELICENSED industries under IDR Act 1951; it is NOT a comprehensive census of all industrial projects and excludes the small number of compulsorily-licensed items and MSME/Udyam registrations. So IEM counts UNDERCOUNT total industrial project starts.

Most recent report located is September 2024 (data through Aug 2024). No 2025-dated IEM Statistics Report was found via search as of 2026-07-22; DPIIT may publish newer months on dpiit.gov.in > Publications/Statistics or via the CMS API. Note DPIIT migrated IEM services to NSWS (National Single Window System) per PIB PRID 2173538, which may affect where the newest statistics are posted.

Same monthly report also gives the current single-month industry-wise snapshots: Aug 2024 Part A top industries = Electricals Equipment 18, Chemicals (other than fertilizers) 10, Others 9, Food Processing 7, Drugs & Pharma 6, Metallurgical 5; Aug 2024 Part B top = Food Processing 18, Textiles 9, Electricals Equipment 7, Fermentation 5, Sugar 5, Chemicals 4.
unavailable: No 2025-dated IEM Statistics Report retrieved (latest found = September 2024, covering data up to Aug 2024). Newer monthly reports, if published, would extend the same industry-wise tables.

DPIIT Annual Report 2024-25 / 2025-26 appendices were not separately opened; the monthly IEM Statistics Report annexures already provide the same industry-wise Part A and Part B counts with more granularity and recency, so they were used as the primary source.
A NIC 2-digit crosswalk is NOT provided by DPIIT for these IEM categories; mapping IEM broad-groups to EIA Schedule codes must be done manually (indicative mapping supplied in notes/eia_ec_relevant).
Exact one-to-one EC-activity concordance not derivable from DPIIT alone (IEM category granularity differs from EIA Schedule).
